learningGroup.title: Learning Group
learningGroup.hint: While a learning group is active, only that group's content is shown everywhere (programs, poses, and in block programming). New programs and poses automatically belong to the active group. The resting pose is always available.
learningGroup.deleteHint: Deleting a group keeps its programs/poses, which become generally visible again.

autoOff.title: Automatic Rest Mode
autoOff.hint: After this much time without movement, the robot automatically moves to the resting pose and then cuts motor power - prevents overheating if it's accidentally left switched on. Leave empty or 0 to disable.
autoOff.label: Auto-off time (minutes)

maxSpeed.title: Maximum movement speed
maxSpeed.hint: Caps the movement speed for the whole robot (safety limit). The speed slider under Poses only goes up to this value; a higher speed is automatically clamped to this limit.
maxSpeed.label: Maximum speed

display.title: Eye Display
display.hint: If the eyes aren't fully shown in fullscreen after booting the robot, restarting the display helps.

language.title: Language
language.hint: Sets the language of the entire interface (menus, buttons, blocks in the program editor). Changes take effect immediately and apply to this browser.
language.label: Language

chat.title: Chat Connection
chat.hint: Determines which service talks to the voice assistant and chat - applies globally to all personalities. Only one option is active at a time; the matching fields appear below.
chat.geminiHint: Without a valid key, the voice assistant can't be activated with Gemini. "Verify" tests the key directly with Google without saving it first.
chat.localLlmHint: Address of an OpenAI-compatible LLM server on the local network (e.g. Ollama, LM Studio) - needs no internet connection and no token.
chat.passwordHint: Enter a password to connect to the Smart API.
chat.encryptIntro: To connect to the Smart API, enter the token you received and encrypt it with a password.
chat.encryptOutro: If you forget the password, you'll need to request a new token.
chat.passwordLengthError: Password must be at least 8 characters long.
chat.encryptFailed: Encryption failed. Please check your network connection and try again.

menu.title: Menu Items
menu.hint: Hides main menu items from the left navigation. Hidden pages remain reachable via their direct web address.
menu.systemLinkLabel: Direct link to the System section:
menu.systemLinkWarning: Once "System" is hidden, the menu item disappears from navigation - this link stays the only way back. Best to copy and save it now.
menu.systemHidesJointLimitsHint: Note: hiding "System" also hides "Set joint limits" under Joint Control → All Joints.

programManager.invalidProgramFile: Not a valid pib program file.

voiceAssistant.toggleAlt: Switch to turn the voice assistant on/off
voiceAssistant.configureConnectionHint: Configure a connection in Settings to start the Voice Assistant
voiceAssistant.personaNamePlaceholder: Describe the persona with a prompt…
voiceAssistant.pauseThresholdAlt: Waiting period without voice input before the voice assistant starts to answer
voiceAssistant.messageHistoryAlt: The number of messages sent to the LLM for better answers
voiceAssistant.smartApiTokenTooltip: Set up a Smart API token in Settings, or choose a model that needs no token (Gemini / local network LLM)
voiceAssistant.movementAccessLabel: Allowed to move pib while speaking (Gemini only) - caution: no preview, the robot moves immediately

rgbLedButton.headline: Please assign a Blockly program to each button.

saveConfirmation.title: Save your changes?
saveConfirmation.message: You have unsaved changes in your program. Do you want to save them before leaving?

console.finishedWithErrors: Program has finished with errors (exit code: {{code}})
console.interrupted: Program execution has been interrupted by the user.
console.executing: Program is now executing (output will be displayed here)
console.notStarted: Program has not started yet (click "Run" to start execution)

motionCapture.motorsActiveHint: ACTIVE - the robot moves along! Turning it off stops it immediately at the current position.
motionCapture.motorsInactiveHint: Off - the robot stays still. Turning it on moves the arms quickly into your current pose.
motionCapture.debugStatus: Debug: MediaPipe ready: {{ready}} | Frames received: {{received}} | Hands detected: {{hands}}
motionCapture.chooseSourceHint: Choose a source and click Start
motionCapture.calibrateTooltip: Steps through which detected body side controls which joint
motionCapture.jointClickHint: Click a joint to include it in the movement transfer. Values appear once capture is running and a person is detected; the rotation joints need a bent arm.
motionCapture.mappingHint: Color and source side per row show which joint point in the video currently drives that joint. "Low"/"High": move the joint into that extreme position and click "Use Current" - maps the motion onto the motor's full range. "Limit Min/Max" can further rein that range in if needed. Every change saves immediately.
motionCapture.calibrateLowTooltip: Move the joint to its lower extreme, then click - maps onto the motor's lower limit
motionCapture.calibrateHighTooltip: Move the joint to its upper extreme, then click - maps onto the motor's upper limit
motionCapture.limitUseCurrentTooltip: Use the actual current motor position as this limit
motionCapture.calibrationHint: Move around and watch which of the two values below reacts - then click the matching side. If the value reacts in the wrong direction, also check "Invert".

voiceRecording.insecureContextWarning: Microphone recording in the browser needs a secure connection (HTTPS or localhost). pib runs here over http:// on the local network, so the browser blocks microphone access by default.
voiceRecording.uploadStillWorks: Uploading finished .wav or .mp3 files still works normally (see below).
voiceRecording.workaround: Workaround for Chrome/Edge: open chrome://flags/#unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure, enter this address ({{origin}}), enable it, and restart the browser.
voiceRecording.chooseFile: Choose a WAV or MP3 file
voiceRecording.playOnPibTooltip: Play the recording on pib's speaker
voiceRecording.insecureConnectionError: Recording needs a secure connection (HTTPS or localhost) - the browser blocks microphone access over http://.

facialExpression.title: Manage Facial Expressions
facialExpression.description: Upload your own GIF files as additional facial expressions - they'll then show up on the Pose page under "Facial expression".

jointControl.selectTouchpoint: Select a touch point to adjust.
jointControl.speedToggleAlt: Switch to toggle between speed and extended values
jointControl.sliderRangeError: {{name}} must be a number and can not be empty, minus, or greater than {{max}}
jointControl.motorToggleAlt: Switch to turn the motor power on/off

allJointsPage.mirrorTooltip: When dragging a slider, automatically moves the matching joint on the other side of the body along - handy for building symmetric poses.
allJointsPage.jointLimitsTooltip: Shows "set as min/max" buttons next to every slider to take over the joint's current position as its new rotation limit.
allJointsPage.jointLimitsHint: Move the joint close to (but not all the way to) its mechanical stop - watch the motor current, it rises sharply right before the stop - then click "set as min" or "set as max" here. The limit applies globally from then on: to manual control, poses and programs alike, preventing unnatural over-rotation. If the slider can't reach far enough anymore (because a limit is already set), use "−5°"/"+5°" to loosen it first.
allJointsPage.limitErrorMin: Minimum must be smaller than the current maximum.
allJointsPage.limitErrorMax: Maximum must be larger than the current minimum.
allJointsPage.widenTooltip: Widens this limit by 5° so the slider can reach further - watch the motor current, then drag the slider and click "set as min/max" to commit the new value.
allJointsPage.hint: Columns are mirrored (facing the robot): the left side controls its right side, the right side its left. The hand slider opens and closes all fingers of that hand together; below it, each finger can be controlled individually. The checkbox in front of each slider only inverts its slide direction (purely visual, doesn't affect the robot's actual movement).

ipRetriever.qrHint: Scan to open the interface on another device

camera.micTooltip: Play the robot's microphone audio in the browser

hardwareId.headline: Please enter the bricklet UIDs.
hardwareId.validationError: IDs must be unique, up to 6 characters long, and contain only letters and numbers.

programAssignment.headline: Assign programs and poses to learning groups.
programAssignment.hint: A program or pose can only belong to one group. To move it, just switch the group in the dropdown - to copy it to another group, pick the target group on the right and click "Copy". Gestures and movement sequences aren't included here yet.
programAssignment.name: Name

pose.deleteAllWarning: This will irrevocably delete all poses you created (default poses are kept). Tip: run "Export all" first as a backup.
pose.mathPrompt: To confirm, please solve this in your head:
pose.poseSourceTooltip: Where the camera images for detection come from
pose.debugStatus: Debug: MediaPipe ready: {{ready}} | Frames received: {{received}} | Frames drawn: {{drawn}}
pose.invalidSequenceFile: Not a valid movement sequence file.
pose.trackingStartFailed: Gesture recognition could not be started (see browser console).

pinAssignment.headline: Servo Bricklet Pin Layout
pinAssignment.hint: For each pin you can pick the connected body part or set it to "not connected". A body part can only ever be assigned to one pin - it automatically disappears from the other dropdowns. Defective (e.g. burnt-out) pins can be marked once no body part is attached to them anymore - set "not connected" first. Changes only take effect after the ros-motors container is restarted.
